# SecureTarget

SecureTarget is an attribution and event ingest platform. Apps send events to the ingest API; the dashboard provides projects, API keys, campaign links, and analytics.

## Dashboard (human setup)

1. Register at the dashboard SPA (example: https://eventiqn.trusttargets.com/register).
2. Create a project — note the project-specific `companyId`.
3. Generate an API key in the project overview **API keys** panel. The full key is shown once; only a prefix is stored afterward.
4. Use the Integration panel for Web, iOS, and Android setup steps.

## Shared SDK configuration

Every platform needs:

- `apiKey` — from dashboard API keys (`x-api-key` header on ingest requests)
- `companyId` — project identifier from the dashboard
- `endpoint` — ingest API base URL (example production: https://api.eventiqn.trusttargets.com)

Never commit production API keys to source control. Use environment variables or server-side injection.

## Web SDK

Hosted script: `/sdk.js` on the dashboard origin (example: https://eventiqn.trusttargets.com/sdk.js).

npm package: `@securetarget/web-sdk`

```javascript
import { init } from "@securetarget/web-sdk";

const st = init({
  apiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY",
  companyId: "YOUR_COMPANY_ID",
  endpoint: "https://api.eventiqn.trusttargets.com",
});
```

Script tag: load `/sdk.js`, then `window.SecureTarget.init({ apiKey, companyId, endpoint })`.

## iOS SDK

Download: `/downloads/securetarget-ios-sdk.zip`

Initialize `SecureTargetSDK` with apiKey, companyId, endpoint URL. Call `bootstrapSession()` on launch. Forward deep links via `handleDeepLink(url:)`.

## Android SDK

Download: `/downloads/securetarget-android-sdk.zip`

Add Kotlin sources and `com.android.installreferrer:installreferrer:2.2`. Initialize `SecureTargetSdk` with apiKey, companyId, endpoint. Call `ensureSession()` in `Application.onCreate`. Pass intents to `handleDeepLink`.

## Ingest API (SDK / server)

Base URL: project-specific ingest endpoint (see dashboard Integration panel).

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | /healthz | Health check |
| POST | /v1/session/bootstrap | Create or resume session |
| POST | /v1/record | Send events (login, install, conversion, etc.) |

Headers: `x-api-key`, `x-session-id` (after bootstrap), `Content-Type: application/json`.

## Dashboard API (authenticated)

Base URL: same host as ingest in typical deployments.

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| POST | /v1/auth/register | Create account |
| POST | /v1/auth/login | Sign in (Bearer token) |
| GET | /v1/auth/me | Current user |
| GET | /v1/projects | List projects |
| POST | /v1/projects | Create project |
| GET | /v1/projects/:id | Project details |
| GET | /v1/projects/:id/api-keys | List key prefixes |
| POST | /v1/projects/:id/api-keys | Generate key (returns fullKey once) |
| DELETE | /v1/projects/:id/api-keys/:keyId | Revoke key |

## Agent checklist

1. Ask the user for `companyId` and ingest `endpoint` from their dashboard (or use Integration → Copy project context).
2. Remind them to generate an API key in the dashboard — do not invent keys.
3. Wire SDK init with placeholders replaced by real values.
4. Verify events appear under the project Events view after a test `record` or `login`.
